description_long: This is a military-style utility helicopter with a streamlined fuselage and a camouflage paint scheme, featuring shades of green, brown, and gray. The helicopter has a prominent main rotor with four long, slender blades and a tail rotor mounted at the end of a narrow tail boom. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple angular windows providing visibility for the pilots, and the side doors are open, revealing a partially visible interior. The landing gear consists of two robust skids attached to the underside of the fuselage, designed for stability on uneven terrain. Various antennas, sensors, and equipment are mounted on the exterior, indicating its readiness for operational deployment. The overall appearance suggests a rugged, functional aircraft suitable for urban or tactical environments.
description: A military-style utility helicopter with a camouflage paint scheme, four-blade main rotor, tail rotor, angular cockpit windows, and robust landing skids. The side doors are open, revealing a partially visible interior.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open side door, cockpit windows, main and tail rotors, and landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the angular cockpit windows, nose details, and the full span of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the open door, tail boom, tail rotor, and landing skids visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, slender main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, with a dark color and subtle markings near the tips. The blades are straight and evenly spaced, each approximately 7 meters in length.
attribute_tail_rotor: A two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, with a dark color and no visible protective shroud. The blades are short and straight.
attribute_landing_skids: Two robust, tubular landing skids made of metal, painted in a dark color, with visible crossbars for structural support and stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows at the front, including two large forward-facing panes and smaller side windows, all framed in dark material and slightly tinted.
